description.summary: The Montauk Project is a conspiracy theory alleging that secret U.S. government experiments in time travel, teleportation, mind control and contact with extraterrestrials were conducted at Camp Hero / Montauk Air Force Station on Long Island, New York; the claims, originating with Preston Nichols and Peter Moon in the early 1990s, are widely characterised as fiction.
description.full: The Montauk Project is a conspiracy theory that grew out of stories circulating in the early 1980s and was popularised by the book series begun in 1992 by Preston Nichols and Peter Moon (real name Vincent Barbarick). The project is alleged to have taken place at Camp Hero / Montauk Air Force Station, located at Montauk Point, Long Island, New York (approximately 41.0622 N, 71.8739 W). Claims include time travel, teleportation, psychological warfare, mind control programs, contact with extraterrestrials and even alleged involvement in faking the Apollo Moon landings, framed as a continuation of the alleged 'Philadelphia Experiment' of 1943. Nichols and his collaborator Al Bielek claimed they had recovered repressed memories of involvement. The work has been characterised as fiction (UFO researcher Jacques Vallée called the accounts 'highly questionable', and the authors themselves told readers the books could be read 'as science fiction or non-fiction'). No documentary or physical evidence supports the claims. Camp Hero State Park does exist on the site of the former Air Force station and is open to the public; its abandoned radar tower is the most-photographed Montauk Project icon. The conspiracy theory has had significant pop-culture influence, including being the working-title basis for Netflix's Stranger Things (2016-2025) and the subject of the documentary Montauk Chronicles (2015).

etymology.origin: Named for Montauk, the easternmost hamlet of Long Island, New York, and specifically for Montauk Air Force Station / Camp Hero located there.
etymology.meaning: 'The Montauk Project' is the name given to the alleged secret program by Preston Nichols' book series.
etymology.first_use: Stories circulating since the early 1980s; book series begun by Nichols and Peter Moon in 1992.

related_phenomena.name: Philadelphia Experiment
related_phenomena.description: Earlier conspiracy theory presented as a precursor to the Montauk Project in Nichols' books

related_phenomena.name: Area 51
related_phenomena.description: Real military site that has accreted similar conspiracy claims

related_phenomena.name: Moon landing hoax
related_phenomena.description: Conspiracy theory the Montauk Project literature also incorporates by claiming Montauk staged the Apollo landings

physical.morphology: Not a creature: an alleged secret-program conspiracy theory rather than a physical entity.

habitat_preference: alleged operations centred on Camp Hero / Montauk Air Force Station, Long Island, New York

range.description: Allegedly centred at Camp Hero / Montauk Air Force Station on the eastern tip of Long Island, New York. As a conspiracy theory it has spread globally through Nichols' and Moon's books and subsequent media adaptations.

hotspots.name: Camp Hero State Park / former Montauk Air Force Station

location.description: Camp Hero State Park, Montauk Point, Long Island

hotspots.peak_period: Alleged operations claimed to have taken place from 1971 onward
hotspots.notes: The decommissioned military radar tower at Camp Hero is the icon of the Montauk Project legend

first_recorded.source: Stories circulating since the early 1980s, per Wikipedia

peak_activity_periods.description: Publication of the Nichols / Moon Montauk Project book series and related conspiracy literature
Pop-culture revival via Montauk Chronicles (2015) and Stranger Things (2016-2025)

origins.cultural_roots: 1980s American UFO and conspiracy subculture; the alleged Philadelphia Experiment narrative.
origins.earliest_accounts: Stories circulating in the 1980s; first published as a book series in 1992.
origins.folklore_connections: Sits within a wider American 'secret base / hidden program' conspiracy tradition that includes Area 51 and Dulce Base.

timeline.event: Stories about an alleged secret program at Montauk begin to circulate within UFO subcultures
Preston Nichols and Peter Moon publish 'The Montauk Project: Experiments in Time', the first book in the series
timeline.significance: Founding text of the modern conspiracy theory

timeline.event: Camp Hero opens to the public as Camp Hero State Park
timeline.significance: Real public access to the alleged site
timeline.source: New York State Parks records (per Wikipedia)

timeline.event: Documentary 'Montauk Chronicles' wins best documentary at the Philip K. Dick Film Festival
Netflix's Stranger Things, with the working title 'Montauk', premieres
timeline.significance: Major pop-culture diffusion of Montauk Project tropes

history.modern_era: The Montauk Project has had limited engagement from mainstream journalism or academic historians, who treat it as fiction. Its main cultural footprint today is via Stranger Things and via paranormal-podcast and conspiracy-literature ecosystems. Camp Hero State Park is a real, well-documented decommissioned Cold War radar installation that draws visitors partly because of the Montauk Project legend.

paranormal.name: Nichols / Bielek 'recovered memory' account
paranormal.description: Preston Nichols and Al Bielek claimed to have recovered repressed memories of participation in time travel, teleportation, mind control, extraterrestrial contact, and other operations at Montauk Air Force Station.
evidence_cited: Recovered-memory testimony from Nichols and Bielek
Continuity claimed with the alleged Philadelphia Experiment

skeptical.name: Fictional / unsupported claims
skeptical.description: The work has been characterised as fiction; UFO researcher Jacques Vallée called the accounts 'highly questionable', and the authors themselves told readers the books could be read 'as science fiction or non-fiction'. No documentary or physical evidence supports the claims.
evidence_cited: Authors' own framing of the work
Discrepancies with declassified Camp Hero history

traditions: American UFO and 'secret base' conspiracy tradition
stories: Stories of 'Montauk Boys' kidnapped for psychological experiments
folklore.beliefs: Belief in the Montauk Project is concentrated in conspiracy and paranormal subcultures and is widely rejected by historians and journalists.

literature: The Montauk Project: Experiments in Time — Preston Nichols and Peter Moon (1992)
Subsequent volumes in the Montauk Project book series (Sky Books, 1990s-2000s)
film_tv: Montauk Chronicles (2015 documentary; best documentary, Philip K. Dick Film Festival)
Stranger Things (Netflix, 2016-2025; working title 'Montauk', inspired by the conspiracy)
other: Camp Hero State Park: real public park on the site of the former Montauk Air Force Station

research.ongoing_studies: No ongoing mainstream research; the Montauk Project is treated as a conspiracy-theory case study rather than a research program.

sources.title: The Montauk Project: Experiments in Time
